title: Day 9 — Skills + On-Demand Knowledge
subtitle: This map keeps Day 9's four main lines: skill catalog discovery, on-demand loading, the /skill allowed_tools lifecycle, and the output-style layer.

diagrams.label: Skill catalog
diagrams.title: Why only name + description at startup
diagrams.summary: build_system_prompt scans .agent/skills/*/SKILL.md and injects only the <available-skills> catalog card into the system prompt. Bodies stay out of resident context; broken skills are skipped with warnings.

nodes.label: CLI Runtime
nodes.detail: The CLI resolves cwd, then enters the Agent Loop or slash dispatch. Skills live under .agent/skills/ relative to cwd.

nodes.label: .agent/skills
nodes.detail: Each skill is a subdirectory plus SKILL.md. Frontmatter holds name, description, allowed_tools; the body is the workflow text.

nodes.label: SkillLoader
nodes.detail: A hand-written frontmatter parser avoids PyYAML. Skills missing name or description are skipped so one bad file cannot break the CLI.

nodes.label: build_system_prompt
nodes.detail: The system prompt is assembled in a fixed order. Day 9 appends the skill catalog after memory, not the body text.

nodes.label: /skills
nodes.detail: Slash dispatch calls SkillLoader.render_list() directly and prints name + description to the terminal without a model request.

nodes.label: <available-skills>
nodes.detail: The model knows which playbooks exist, not the full four-step debug flow. With 20 skills, the catalog is a light index; bodies load on demand.

diagrams.label: On-demand load
diagrams.title: Two entry paths: skill_load vs /skill
diagrams.summary: skill_list / skill_load are read-only tools: the model reads a playbook and the body returns through tool_result without changing RuntimeState.skill_allowed_tools. /skill is the user assigning this turn's task and injecting the body into the prompt.

nodes.label: /skill <name>
nodes.detail: Slash reads the body, builds a prompt with Task, and returns should_query=True. From v3 onward it also carries allowed_tools.

nodes.label: user prompt
nodes.detail: A normal prompt enters the Agent Loop. The system prompt already contains the <available-skills> catalog, so the model may choose skill_load on its own.

nodes.label: Provider
nodes.detail: The model sees skill_list and skill_load as read-only tool schemas and uses the same tool_use protocol as other tools.

nodes.label: skill_list
nodes.detail: Shares SkillLoader with /skills and returns name + description text to the model.

nodes.label: skill_load
nodes.detail: Loads the SKILL.md body on demand and backfills it as a tool_result into messages. Does not change the tool whitelist — reading a manual is not accepting a job.

nodes.label: messages
nodes.detail: The skill_load body enters message history for later turns. Day 8 emit does not print observation lines to the terminal.

diagrams.label: Tool boundary
diagrams.title: Why allowed_tools lives for one turn only
diagrams.summary: allowed_tools from /skill is wrapped in AgentJob; the worker sets state.skill_allowed_tools before run_turn and restores it in finally. ToolRegistry.filtered plus PermissionRequest deny form a double gate against out-of-bounds tool calls.

nodes.label: SlashResult
nodes.detail: The slash handler runs on the main thread and does not mutate shared state directly. The whitelist travels with the next job so an in-flight turn is not polluted.

nodes.label: AgentJob
nodes.detail: Day 8 job_queue stored str; Day 9 upgrades to a dataclass. Normal input uses allowed_tools=None.

nodes.label: worker thread
nodes.detail: After dequeuing a job, the worker saves the old whitelist, sets job.allowed_tools, runs run_turn, then restores in finally so later normal chat is not stuck read-only.

nodes.label: ToolRegistry.filtered
nodes.detail: provider.complete only sees schemas inside the whitelist. debug-test allows read_file / grep; the model cannot see file_edit.

nodes.label: execute_one_tool_call
nodes.detail: Execution also uses the filtered registry; hidden write tools cannot be reached from the original ToolRegistry.

nodes.label: PermissionRequest
nodes.detail: Even if the model emits an out-of-bounds tool call from history, decide_permission denies it: skill allowed_tools does not allow X.

diagrams.label: Output style
diagrams.title: Why style stays separate from skills
diagrams.summary: Skills hold task knowledge (how to debug, how to release). output-style holds answer style (more explanation, code-review tone). /output-style mutates RuntimeState.output_style; each rebuilt system prompt appends <output-style> last without changing tool permissions.

nodes.label: .agent/output-styles
nodes.detail: Same .agent family as skills, different semantics: how to speak, not what task to run.

nodes.label: /output-style
nodes.detail: Interactive shell only. use sets state.output_style; reset clears to None. Not persisted after process exit.

nodes.label: RuntimeState
nodes.detail: Lives beside skill_allowed_tools in RuntimeState; main thread writes, worker reads.

nodes.label: build_system_prompt
nodes.detail: Day 9 fixes a latent bug: the system prompt cannot be built once at CLI startup. Both the skills catalog and style are runtime variables.

nodes.label: bottom_toolbar
nodes.detail: The prompt_toolkit status bar shows the active style; it is not a normal stdout line.

nodes.label: <output-style>
nodes.detail: Last because style shapes expression; it must not override AGENT.md rules or permission boundaries.
